design_decisions.description: Terminal-set semantics for the resume seed = {verified, shipped, deferred} (the router's definition); implemented is NON-terminal. Replaces the original AC2 ('skip past implemented'), which would have abandoned implemented-but-unverified slices because the frontier walk is forward-only.
design_decisions.rationale: The router already treats implemented as non-terminal (routeImplemented drives implemented -> /verify-slice). A divergent seed that skips implemented lands on a later slice the forward-only router can never return to, so a crashed-mid-verify slice is abandoned. Seeding at implemented re-enters verification, recovering the work.

design_decisions.description: Unify the terminal-set in one exported router.IsTerminal helper consumed by both router (router.go:307, :393) and the scheduler seed. No divergent terminal definition in the scheduler.
design_decisions.rationale: The router comment at worker.go:533-535 already declares the router the single source of truth for the state machine. A second terminal definition in the scheduler contradicts that and risks drift (the original design dropped 'deferred' and added 'implemented'). One helper, two consumers.

design_decisions.description: findFirstNonTerminal signature gains the oracle reader + release/track context (already in scope at runTrackRouter). Reversible, local refactor.
design_decisions.rationale: The seed must read committed state; the oracle reader is already constructed and wired into WorkerOptions (parallel.go:183/275/337), so threading it to the seed is minimal.
